notes.introduction: An exposition of Genesis 17:1 on the name El Shaddai, the first occurrence of that divine name in the Old Testament, tracing God's self-revelation to Abram as the Almighty God and the corresponding demand for a complete, wholehearted walk before Him. The study extends into Ephesians 4-5 to draw out five descriptions of the believer's walk as a practical outworking of salvation.

sections.heading: El Shaddai: First Occurrence and Meaning
Thirteen Silent Years Before the Greeting
What God Declares: Revelation of the Almighty
What God Demands: Walk and Be Perfect
Five Aspects of the Believer's Walk in Ephesians
Meditating on God's Identity Amid Present Chaos
